title: Oracle Data Deep Dive NYC: AI Database as the Foundation for Agentic AI — A New Architectural Paradigm
summary: On April 15, Oracle held Oracle Data Deep Dive NYC in New York — an event focused on its AI database convergence strategy for enterprise agentic AI. Oracle bets on a unified engine approach: vector search, graph, JSON, relational, and agent memory — without purpose-built AI silos. Exadata Exascale uses Raft replication for sub-3-second zero-data-loss failover designed specifically for agentic workloads. Oracle challenges the 'orchestration above data' paradigm: agent logic should execute as close to the data as possible, not above fragmented data stores.

title: Oracle EPM April 2026 (26.04): Fusion AI Planning Agent — From Quarterly Cycles to Event-Driven FP&A
summary: Oracle's April 2026 Fusion Cloud EPM update (26.04) integrates Fusion AI Agents into financial planning workflows. The Planning Agent shifts FP&A teams from quarterly cycles to event-driven planning: real-time variance and trend analysis via natural language, what-if simulations on Fusion ERP/EPM data, and automated narrative reporting. New REST APIs enable programmatic agentic AI interaction with EPM Cloud. Predictive Cash Forecasting — previously available only in beta — is now open to all customers.

title: Qualys VMDR & TotalCloud Now on Oracle Cloud Marketplace: One-Click OCI Security
summary: On April 15, Qualys announced availability of VMDR (Vulnerability Management, Detection and Response) and TotalCloud CNAPP on the Oracle Cloud Marketplace. The solution delivers unified visibility across OCI cloud, containers, workloads, and applications with risk-based vulnerability prioritization. One-click provisioning via native OCI integration. Customers can use existing OCI committed spend — no separate procurement process required.

title: OCI Java Management Service: JDK 26 Support — Oracle First Public Cloud Provider
summary: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Java Management Service added support for JDK 26, released March 17, 2026. OCI became the first public cloud provider to offer Oracle JDK 26 — featuring AI integration, new security tooling, and performance improvements. Oracle also announced the Java Verified Portfolio (JVP) — a curated set of Oracle-supported tools, frameworks, and libraries, free for Java SE subscribers and OCI customers running Java workloads on OCI.

title: Oracle Financial Services Extends Agentic AI Platform to Corporate Banking: Agents for Loans, Guarantees & SCF
summary: At the Oracle Financial Services Summit on April 14, Oracle expanded its agentic AI platform to corporate banking. New pre-built AI agents cover the full cycle: Loan Data Extraction Agent processes lengthy credit agreements, Financial Data Validation Agent verifies financial inputs, Application Validator Agent handles bank guarantee applications, and SCF Program Creation Agent launches supply chain finance programs. Built with human-in-the-loop governance. Oracle plans 100+ corporate and retail banking agents within 12 months.

title: Oracle Primavera Unifier Gets AI Agents for Capital Project Management — Oracle Stock +6.5%
summary: On April 14, Oracle announced an AI overhaul for Primavera Unifier, its flagship capital project management platform. New capabilities: AI workflow summaries, no-code/low-code process automation via Oracle Integration, real-time adapters and triggers, improved reporting dashboards, and audit-ready compliance. Oracle Integration gives AI agents secure, fully auditable access to enterprise data. Markets responded with Oracle stock surging 6.5% on April 14, 2026.

title: Oracle AI World Tour Singapore: SMRT Deploys OCI Enterprise AI for Rail Maintenance Intelligence
summary: At Oracle AI World Tour Singapore on April 14, SMRT — Singapore's leading public transportation provider — announced a pilot using OCI Enterprise AI and Oracle Autonomous AI Database. The JARVIS platform (built by STRIDES Technologies) unifies operational data from multiple systems and applies AI for rail infrastructure condition analysis via a generative AI chatbot interface. The case demonstrates Oracle AI applicability in mission-critical industrial infrastructure.

title: Oracle Financial Crime & Compliance: New AI Agents for Suspicious Transaction Detection and Compliance
summary: On April 9, Oracle announced enhancements to the Oracle FCCM (Financial Crime and Compliance Management) platform with Lucinity technology and new AI agents. Agents automate detection of suspicious financial transactions, compliance verification, and transaction monitoring. For banks and financial institutions migrating from legacy systems, Oracle now offers a fully AI-native financial compliance infrastructure.

title: OCI Enterprise AI Goes GA: Oracle Unifies AI Models, Agents, and Governance in One Platform
summary: On March 26, Oracle announced the general availability of OCI Enterprise AI — a platform combining OCI Enterprise AI Models (frontier model access), OCI Enterprise AI Agents (build and deploy agentic workloads using managed tools and open-source frameworks), and OCI Enterprise AI Governance (enterprise-grade security and compliance). OpenAI Responses API compatibility lets teams reuse existing code patterns. On April 15, Oracle hosts an introductory webinar.

title: OCI Compute: AMD Instinct MI355X GPUs — Oracle First Public Hyperscaler
summary: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure became the first public cloud provider to offer AMD Instinct MI355X GPUs. OCI Supercluster scales to 131,072 GPUs. Each MI355X carries 288 GB of HBM3E memory at 8 TB/s bandwidth — nearly 3x more powerful than the previous generation. In Q3 2026, Oracle and AMD plan to deploy an AMD Instinct MI450 Series supercluster with 50,000 GPUs.

title: Ukraine Bill 13505: Up to 2% Revenue Fine for Failing to Migrate from 1C and BAS
summary: Ukraine's draft law No. 13505 includes a direct financial penalty: a fine of up to 2% of annual revenue for companies that do not complete migration by the set deadlines. Banks and investors already have grounds to refuse financing to companies whose policies reflect continued use of 'hostile software.' Over 300,000 Ukrainian companies are estimated to still run on 1C or BAS.

title: APEXlang in APEX 26.1: ACE Community Explains the DevOps Approach to Low-Code
summary: The Oracle ACE Program published its April 2026 APEXlang must-read roundup. MaxAPEX's blog breaks down the .apx format in depth: a human-readable YAML/JSON for building complete APEX applications with git version control and CI/CD support. APEXlang AI Copilot enables generating and editing applications in natural language. Community consensus: APEX 26.1 is a qualitative leap in DevOps-native low-code development.

title: APEX Alpe Adria 2026: Oracle APEX Conference in Ljubljana, April 16–17
summary: The Oracle APEX community conference organized by Slovenia, Croatia, and Austria takes place April 16–17 at Four Points by Sheraton Ljubljana. Agenda covers APEXlang, new APEX 26.1 features, AI Copilot, and DevOps integration. Part of the growing wave of APEX community events in spring 2026.

title: OCI Autonomous AI Database: Critical Wallet Update Required by April 15
summary: Oracle warns: DigiCert is revoking trust in G1 root certificates on April 15, 2026. Users of Oracle Autonomous AI Database Serverless and Essbase on OCI Marketplace must download a new wallet (issued after January 28, 2026) before the deadline to avoid losing database connectivity.

title: Oracle APEX Bootcamps North America: Free Tour Covering APEX 26.1 & APEXlang
summary: Oracle is running a series of free one-day APEX Bootcamps from April 1 to May 7 across five cities in the US, Canada, and Mexico (Boston, Reston, Minneapolis, Chicago, Guadalajara). Attendees get access to OCI Gen AI and explore APEXlang and the APEX 26.1 approach to AI-driven development.

title: APEX Connect 2026: 12th Annual Oracle APEX Conference at Heide Park Soltau
summary: The 12th edition of APEX Connect takes place May 18, 2026, at Heide Park Soltau, Germany. Organized by DOAG, the leading Oracle community in the DACH region. Topics: APEXlang, AI in APEX, next-generation Interactive Reports, Map Regions, Dynamic Actions, and PL/SQL APIs.

title: Fusion Agentic Applications for HR: 8 AI Agents in Oracle HCM
summary: Oracle announced 8 new AI-agent applications within Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM. Career Advancement Command Center, Contract Compliance Workspace and others automate HR processes — career mobility, contract compliance, and workforce decisions — inside a unified enterprise platform without third-party integrations.

title: Fusion Agentic Applications for Finance & SCM: 12 AI Agents for ERP and Supply Chains
summary: Oracle introduced 12 new AI-agent applications across Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP and SCM. Claims Settlement Workspace accelerates claim resolution and improves financial accuracy. Agents automate financial operations, supply chain management, and manufacturing within a unified agentic ERP stack.

title: OCI Generative AI: Import NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super 120B and Qwen 3 Embedding Models
summary: OCI Generative AI added support for importing open-weights models: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super 120B (high-performance reasoning and instruction following) and Qwen 3 Embedding (text embedding and reranking for enterprise RAG scenarios). Models are available for deployment within a customer's own OCI tenancy.

title: OCI Cache: Valkey 8 with Native JSON and Vector Similarity Search
summary: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Cache announced support for Valkey 8 with improved I/O threading (up to 3.5x faster for KEYS operations and replication), native JSON module, and Valkey-search for high-performance vector similarity search. New capabilities open semantic search scenarios in cache without additional infrastructure.
